title: d2c Walker Fixtures
description: Per-step fixture values used by the Phase 4a flow-walker spec when filling forms on `validate: form` stepper steps. Lives at <run-dir>/flow/walker-fixtures.json. Auto-fixture values from SKILL.md §4a.2 are written here on first use; values supplied by the user via F-FLOW-WALKER-VALIDATION-BLOCKED are written here too with `supplied_by: "user"` so reruns won't re-prompt. User-supplied values always win over auto-generated values for the same (step, field) tuple.

schema_version.description: Fixture artifact schema version. v1 only in MVP.

fixtures.description: Map of step key (format `<group_node_id>__step_<N>` where N is 1-based) to a per-field map. Empty object when no validate:form steps exist or no auto-fixtures have been generated yet.

additionalProperties.description: Per-field fixture values for one step. Field name keys mirror state_writes[i].name from the step's IR.

fixture_entry.description: A single fixture value with provenance. supplied_by tracks whether the auto-fixture rules from SKILL.md §4a.2 produced this value or whether the user supplied it via F-FLOW-WALKER-VALIDATION-BLOCKED. The walker spec reads user values first, falling back to auto-fixture values; reads of an absent field at runtime trigger a fresh auto-fixture generation.

value.description: The literal value the walker fills into the field. Type depends on the field's state_writes type — string for text fields, number for numeric fields, boolean for checkboxes.

supplied_by.description: Provenance. 'auto-fixture' = generated by SKILL.md §4a.2 rules. 'user' = supplied by the user via F-FLOW-WALKER-VALIDATION-BLOCKED. User-supplied values always win on rewrite (the writer never silently downgrades a 'user' entry to 'auto-fixture').

supplied_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this entry was first written. Lets the user know how stale a fixture is in case the form's validation rules drifted.

field_type.description: Optional — mirrors the matched state_writes[i].type when the auto-fixture writer recorded it. Helps the walker pick the right input setter (fill vs check vs setValue).
